name: Securities and Exchange Commission Nigeria
statute: Rules on Issuance, Offering Platforms and Custody of Digital Assets 2022

obligations.title: Register as a Virtual Asset Service Provider
obligations.description: Operators of digital asset offering platforms, exchanges, and custodians must register with SEC before carrying on business in Nigeria.
obligations.clauseRef: Digital Assets Rules 2022 Part A Rule 1

obligations.title: File quarterly operating and compliance returns
obligations.description: File quarterly returns covering volumes, AUC, open complaints, suspicious activity reports filed, and any regulatory breaches.

obligations.title: Maintain segregated client asset custody
obligations.description: Custody client digital assets in wallets segregated from operational funds, with documented key management and insurance where applicable.
obligations.clauseRef: Digital Assets Rules 2022 Part C Rule 8

obligations.title: Publish investor disclosures for digital-asset offerings
obligations.description: For each digital-asset offering on a Nigerian platform, publish an SEC-approved disclosure document covering risks, rights, custody, and fees before go-live.
obligations.clauseRef: Digital Assets Rules 2022 Part B Rule 4

obligations.title: AML/CFT programme aligned with SEC & NFIU
obligations.description: Operate a written AML/CFT programme covering customer due diligence, transaction monitoring, sanctions screening, and STR filing with NFIU.
obligations.clauseRef: Digital Assets Rules 2022 Rule 15 & AML Act 2022
